front: What is the definition of a sequentially compact metric space?
back: A metric space (X, d) is sequentially compact if every sequence in X has a convergent subsequence that converges to a point in X.

front: What is the relationship between compactness and sequential compactness in metric spaces?
back: In a metric space, the properties are equivalent: a subset is compact if and only if it is sequentially compact.

front: State the Bolzano-Weierstrass Theorem in the context of real numbers.
back: The Bolzano-Weierstrass Theorem states that every bounded sequence in ℝ has a convergent subsequence. This means every closed and bounded interval [a, b] is sequentially compact.

front: How is total boundedness related to sequential compactness?
back: A metric space is sequentially compact if and only if it is complete and totally bounded.

front: True or False: In general topological spaces, sequential compactness always implies compactness.
back: False. While sequential compactness and compactness are equivalent in metric spaces, this is not true for all topological spaces. Compactness implies sequential compactness only in spaces that are first-countable.
